text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, more organized, and easier to understand. By removing unnecessary complexity, renaming unclear variables, or breaking long functions into smaller parts, developers increase readability for themselves and their team. Refactoring also helps reduce technical debt—the hidden cost of messy or quick-fix code. Over time, unrefactored code becomes harder to maintain, slows down development, and leads to more bugs. By regularly refactoring, teams keep their codebase healthy, making future changes faster and safer.
